---
title: "Education Inequality"
author: "Hannah Barrow"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  flexdashboard::flex_dashboard:
    orientation: columns
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
library(flexdashboard)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(knitr)
```

################################################################################
# About

## Left Column

### 

```{r, echo=FALSE}
valueBox("SGD's of Interest:")
```

### 

**[#4: Quality Education](https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal4)** "Ensure inclusive and equitable quality education and promote lifelong learning opportunities for all" 

**[#5: Gender Equality](https://sdgs.un.org/goals/goal5)** "Achieve gender equality and empower all women and girls"

&nbsp; 

#### 

![](../../images/SDG4.png){width="30%"}
![](../../images/SDG5.png){width="30%"}

&nbsp; 

For this data story, I want to explore the differences in education quality and access among countries with different economic levels and practices. To do this, I focused on 8 countries and compared their expenditure on education and inequality. The 8 countries I focused on are Luxembourg, the US, Iceland, Slovakia, Costa Rica, Tuvalu, Somalia, and Burundi (see [map](#map)). I also want to explore gender inequalities in education by looking at how the enrollment of men and women into schools differs and has changed over time. 

################################################################################
# The Data

All datasets came from [Our World In Data](https://ourworldindata.org). 

## Left Column

### [Government spending on education](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/education-spending)

```{r dataset1, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

url <- 'https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/education-spending.csv?v=1&csvType=full&useColumnShortNames=true&spending_type=gdp_share&level=all'

edu_spending_origional <- read_csv(url)

edu_spending <- edu_spending_origional %>% 
  rename(edu_spending_gdp = government_expenditure_on_education_as_a_percentage_of_gdp__pct__xgdp_fsgov)
edu_spending #%>% kable
```

### [Gender gap in primary, secondary and tertiary education](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/gender-gap-education-levels)

```{r dataset2, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

url <- 'https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/gender-gap-education-levels.csv?v=1&csvType=full&useColumnShortNames=true'

edu_gender_origional <- read_csv(url)

edu_gender <- edu_gender_origional %>% 
  rename(f_tertiary = f_tertiary_enrollment_rates_combined_wb,
         f_primary = f_primary_enrollment_rates_combined_wb,
         f_lower_secondary = total_net_enrolment_rate__lower_secondary__female__pct,
         f_upper_secondary = total_net_enrolment_rate__upper_secondary__female__pct,
         m_tertiary = m_tertiary_enrollment_rates_combined_wb,
         m_primary = m_primary_enrollment_rates_combined_wb,
         m_lower_secondary = total_net_enrolment_rate__lower_secondary__male__pct,
         m_upper_secondary = total_net_enrolment_rate__upper_secondary__male__pct)
edu_gender #%>% kable
```

## Right Column

### [Inequality in education](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/inequality-in-education)

```{r dataset3, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

url <- 'https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/inequality-in-education.csv?v=1&csvType=full&useColumnShortNames=true'

edu_equality <- read_csv(url)
edu_equality #%>% kable
```

### [GDP per capita](https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/gdp-per-capita-worldbank?time=2015..latest)

```{r gdp data, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)

url <- 'https://ourworldindata.org/grapher/gdp-per-capita-worldbank.csv?v=1&csvType=full&useColumnShortNames=true'

gdp_origional <- read_csv(url)

gdp_data <- gdp_origional %>% 
  rename(gdp_pcap = ny_gdp_pcap_pp_kd)
gdp_data #%>% kable
```

################################################################################
# Exploring

**GDP per capita compared to spending on education**

## Left Column

### Average GDP per capita over 10 years

```{r GDP world table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

gdp_table <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(gdp_pcap = mean(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  #arrange(desc(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number(desc(gdp_pcap)))
tt(gdp_table)
```

## Left-mid Column

### Average government expenditure on education over 10 years

```{r spending world table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

spending_table <- edu_spending %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(edu_spending_gdp = mean(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  #arrange(desc(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number(desc(edu_spending_gdp)))
tt(spending_table)
```

## Right-mid Column

### Average gender inequality (Atkinson index) over 10 years

```{r inequality world table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

ineq_table <- edu_equality %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(ineq_edu = mean(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  #arrange(desc(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number(desc(ineq_edu)))
tt(ineq_table)
```

## Right Column

### Average enrollment over 10 years

```{r gender edu world table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

gender_edu_table <- edu_gender %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = f_tertiary:f_upper_secondary,
               names_to = "level",
               values_to = "enrollment") %>% 
  summarize(enrollment = mean(enrollment, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  #arrange(desc(enrollment)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number(desc(enrollment)))
tt(gender_edu_table)
```

################################################################################
# Focus Countries

**GDP per capita compared to spending on education**

## Left Column

### Average GDP per capita over 10 years

```{r GDP fc table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

gdp_table_fc <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(gdp_pcap = mean(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(gdp_table_fc)
```

## Left-mid Column

### Average government expenditure on education over 10 years

```{r spending fc table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

spending_table_fc <- edu_spending %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(edu_spending_gdp = mean(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(spending_table_fc)
```

## Right-mid Column

### Average gender inequality (Atkinson index) over 10 years

```{r inequality fc table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

ineq_table_fc <- edu_equality %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(ineq_edu = mean(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(ineq_table_fc)
```

## Right Column

### Average enrollment over 10 years

```{r gender edu fc table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)

gender_edu_fc <- edu_gender %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = f_tertiary:f_upper_secondary,
               names_to = "level",
               values_to = "enrollment") %>% 
  summarize(enrollment = mean(enrollment, na.rm = TRUE)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(enrollment)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(gender_edu_fc)
```

################################################################################
# Spending

## Left Column

### {data-height=35}

```{r GDP filtered table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

gdp_table_fc <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(gdp_pcap = mean(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(gdp_table_fc)
```

### {data-height=65}

```{r GDP line filtered, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

gdp_data_fc <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))

exp_gdp <- ggplot(gdp_data_fc,
                  aes(x = year,
                      y = gdp_pcap,
                      color = entity))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_bw()+
  labs(title = "Average GDP per capita over the past 10 years",
       x = "Country",
       y = "GDP per capita")
ggplotly(exp_gdp)
```

## Right Column

### {data-height=35}

```{r spending filtered table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

spending_table_fc <- edu_spending %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(edu_spending_gdp = mean(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(edu_spending_gdp)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(spending_table_fc)
```

### {data-height=65}

```{r spending line filtered, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

edu_spending_fc <- edu_spending %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))

exp_spend <- ggplot(edu_spending_fc,
                    aes(x = year,
                        y = edu_spending_gdp,
                        color = entity))+
  geom_line()+
  theme_bw()+
  labs(title = "Average government expenditure on education over the past 10 years",
       x = "Country",
       y = "% of GDP for education")
ggplotly(exp_spend)
```

################################################################################
# Inequality

## Left Column

### {data-height=35}

```{r GDP filtered table 2, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

gdp_table_fc <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(gdp_pcap = mean(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(gdp_pcap)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(gdp_table_fc)
```

### {data-height=65}

```{r GDP bar filtered, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

gdp_data_2023 <- gdp_data %>% 
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))

exp_gdp2 <- ggplot(gdp_data_2023, 
                   aes(x = entity,
                       y = gdp_pcap,
                       fill = entity))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))+
  labs(title = "GDP in 2023",
       x = "Country",
       y = "GDP per capita")
ggplotly(exp_gdp2)
```

## Right Column

### {data-height=35}

```{r inequality filtered table, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(tinytable)
library(dplyr)

ineq_table_fc <- edu_equality %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  group_by(entity) %>% 
  summarize(ineq_edu = mean(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  arrange(desc(ineq_edu)) %>% 
  mutate("rank" = row_number()) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))
tt(ineq_table_fc)
```

### {data-height=65}

```{r inequality bar filtered, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)

edu_equality_fc <- edu_equality %>% 
  filter(year == 2023) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo"))

ineq1 <- ggplot(edu_equality_fc,
                aes(x = entity,
                    y = ineq_edu,
                    fill = entity))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))+
  labs(title = "Education inequality in 2023",
       x = "Country",
       y = "Inequality (measured with Atkinson index)")
ggplotly(ineq1)
```

################################################################################
# Gender Edu

## Right Column

### 

```{r gender edu FEMALE, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)

gender_edu_fem <- edu_gender %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo")) %>% 
   pivot_longer(cols = contains("f_"),
               names_to = "female",
               values_to = "f_enrollment") %>% 
   pivot_longer(cols = contains("m_"),
               names_to = "male",
               values_to = "m_enrollment")

fem_col <- ggplot(gender_edu_fem,
       aes(x = year,
           y = f_enrollment,
           fill = entity))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))+
  labs(title = "Female enrollment")
ggplotly(fem_col)
```

## Left Column

### 

```{r gender edu MALE, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(tidyr)

gender_edu_fem <- edu_gender %>% 
  filter(year >= 2014 & year <= 2023) %>% 
  filter(entity %in% c("Burundi","Czechia","Greece","Luxembourg","Guinea","Somalia","Tuvalu","United States","Democratic Republic of Congo")) %>% 
   pivot_longer(cols = contains("f_"),
               names_to = "female",
               values_to = "f_enrollment") %>% 
   pivot_longer(cols = contains("m_"),
               names_to = "male",
               values_to = "m_enrollment")

mal_col <- ggplot(gender_edu_fem,
       aes(x = year,
           y = m_enrollment,
           fill = entity))+
  geom_col()+
  theme_bw()+
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1))+
  labs(title = "Male enrollment")
ggplotly(mal_col)
```

################################################################################
# Map

```{r map, eval=TRUE, echo=FALSE, message=FALSE}
library(leaflet)
url <- "https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1UsY0t3M9jtPlI31Ny8tAQ7gEVrM7Us4iToOzY9rgUX0/edit?usp=sharing"

library(gsheet)
locs <- gsheet2tbl(url)
locs

leaflet() %>% 
  addTiles() %>% 
  addCircleMarkers(data = locs,
             color = locs$marker_color,
             radius = locs$marker_size,
             popup = paste0(locs$location,
                            "<br/>",
                            locs$description))
```

################################################################################
# Sources

1. [Children from the poorest households benefit the least from national public education funding](https://www.unicef.org/eca/press-releases/children-poorest-households-benefit-least-national-public-education-funding-unicef)

2. [Closing the Gap: Girls' Education and Women's Labor](https://blogs.worldbank.org/en/education/closing-gap-tackling-remaining-disparities-girls-education-and-womens-labor-market)

3. [Investing in Quality Education for Economic Development, Peace, and Stability](https://www.csis.org/analysis/investing-quality-education-economic-development-peace-and-stability)

4. [More young women than men have college degrees](https://www.pewresearch.org/short-reads/2024/11/18/us-women-are-outpacing-men-in-college-completion-including-in-every-major-racial-and-ethnic-group/)

5. [New UNESCO Report shows extent of global inequalities in education and calls for greater inclusion as schools re-open](https://www.unesco.org/en/articles/new-unesco-report-shows-extent-global-inequalities-education-and-calls-greater-inclusion-schools-re)
